# Module Boundaries

## Module Map

| Module      | Location              | Responsibilities                                                            |
| ----------- | --------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| AppModule   | `apps/api/src/app/`   | Root module — composes features, configures GraphQL, MongoDB, Redis, Config |
| ItemsModule | `apps/api/src/items/` | CRUD operations for Item entities via GraphQL                               |
| api-e2e     | `apps/api-e2e/`       | End-to-end tests against the running API                                    |

## Module Details

### AppModule

Root module that wires all infrastructure and feature modules.

Configures:

- `ConfigModule.forRoot()` — global env access via `ConfigService`
- `GraphQLModule.forRoot()` — Apollo driver, code-first schema, playground
- `MongooseModule.forRootAsync()` — MongoDB connection from `MONGODB_URI`
- `CacheModule.registerAsync()` — Redis store from `REDIS_HOST`, `REDIS_PORT`, `REDIS_PASSWORD`

Imports: `ItemsModule`

### ItemsModule

Self-contained feature module for Item domain.

Structure:

- `items.resolver.ts` — GraphQL queries (`items`, `item`) and mutations (`createItem`, `updateItem`, `removeItem`)
- `items.service.ts` — Business logic with cache-aside pattern (60s TTL)
- `schemas/item.schema.ts` — Mongoose schema + GraphQL ObjectType (code-first)
- `dto/create-item.input.ts` — Input type for creation
- `dto/update-item.input.ts` — Input type for updates (extends CreateItemInput via PartialType)

Registers: `MongooseModule.forFeature([Item])`

Provides: `ItemsResolver`, `ItemsService`

### api-e2e

End-to-end test project with global setup/teardown lifecycle.

Structure:

- `src/api/api.spec.ts` — API integration tests
- `src/support/global-setup.ts` — Pre-test environment bootstrap
- `src/support/global-teardown.ts` — Post-test cleanup
- `src/support/test-setup.ts` — Per-test configuration

## Dependency Rules

```
Resolver → Service → Mongoose Model
                   → Cache Manager
```

- Resolvers depend only on their module's service
- Services depend on Mongoose models and the cache manager
- Modules do not import other feature modules directly
- Cross-cutting concerns (config, cache) are provided globally by AppModule
- DTOs and schemas are co-located within their feature module

## Shared Concerns

| Concern        | Strategy                                                       |
| -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Configuration  | `@nestjs/config` global module — inject `ConfigService`        |
| Caching        | `@nestjs/cache-manager` global module — inject `CACHE_MANAGER` |
| Validation     | GraphQL input types with `@Field()` decorators                 |
| Error handling | NestJS built-in exception filters (`NotFoundException`)        |
| Logging        | NestJS `Logger` class                                          |
| Testing        | Jest 30 with `@nestjs/testing` — mock models and cache         |
